blocks.content: I'd been there the whole time. Standing a few feet back. Daniel had seen me.
Not when he was focused on Jonathan. But after — when he turned to walk away. His eyes cut to me for half a second. Filing something.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Don't put her name in something like that. That's all. Don't do that.
He was still coming down. I could see it — the tension hadn't fully left his shoulders yet. This was the most heated I'd seen him.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: He's not gonna let it go.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: He can hold on to it as long as he wants. Doesn't change anything.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: He saw me watching the whole time.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: I know he did. That's a Daniel thing — he's gonna come back to you instead, try to find a softer spot. He's not really tough. He's just a person who's good at finding the thing that bothers you and sitting on it.

Daniel had come at me before — tested me in a hallway just like this one. My heart had gone fast. I'd stood there trying to look like I knew what I was doing. Jonathan hadn't thought for a single second.
I still didn't know if that was something you built or something you were born with.

blocks.prompt: Daniel clocked you watching. Jonathan's right — he's going to come back to you.

options.label: "If he comes at me next, I'm telling you first."
"How do you do that? Just — not let it land?"
